routine-maintenance: Ensure the HVAC unit is powered OFF before opening any access panels.
Verify that all capacitors are fully discharged — use a multimeter to confirm zero voltage.
Wear insulated gloves and safety glasses when handling refrigerant lines.
Check for proper ventilation in the work area — refrigerants can displace oxygen.
Secure all panels and fasteners before restoring power to the unit.
critical-fault: IMMEDIATELY disconnect all power sources to the HVAC unit at the breaker panel.
Do NOT approach the unit if you detect the smell of burning insulation or see visible arcing.
Use a lockout/tagout (LOTO) device on the main disconnect switch before proceeding.
Wear full PPE: arc-rated clothing, insulated gloves, safety glasses with side shields, and hard hat.
Test for residual voltage on ALL phases using a certified voltage tester before touching any components.
Have a fire extinguisher (Class C) readily accessible before beginning any work.
If the fault involves refrigerant: evacuate the area and verify the environment is safe before proceeding.

routine-maintenance: Power down the printer completely and disconnect from mains power.
Allow the print head and fuser assembly to cool for at least 10 minutes before handling.
Wear anti-static wrist strap when handling circuit boards or electronic components.
Use only manufacturer-approved cleaning solvents and lint-free wipes for internal cleaning.
Dispose of waste toner and cleaning materials according to local environmental regulations.
critical-fault: Emergency stop the printer immediately using the red E-Stop button (if accessible).
Disconnect the printer from mains power at the wall outlet or breaker panel.
Do NOT open any covers if you see smoke, excessive heat, or hear unusual arcing sounds.
Allow a minimum 15-minute cooldown period for the fuser assembly and internal electronics.
Wear full PPE: heat-resistant gloves, safety glasses, and anti-static footwear.
If a toner spill has occurred, use a HEPA-filtered vacuum — never use a standard vacuum or compressed air.
Document all error codes displayed on the control panel BEFORE resetting or powering off.

routine-maintenance: Verify that the server rack is properly grounded before touching any components.
Wear an anti-static wrist strap connected to the rack's grounding point at all times.
Power down the specific server unit through the OS before disconnecting power cables.
Label all cables before disconnecting — take a photo of the current cable layout for reference.
Handle SAS/SATA drives by the edges only — never touch the connector pins or circuit boards.
critical-fault: Do NOT approach the rack if you smell burning electronics or see smoke — evacuate the area immediately.
If safe to approach, power down the affected server unit using the OS or the power button (hold 10 seconds).
Switch the affected server's PDU outlet to OFF at the rack PDU — do NOT pull the plug under load.
Wait for all internal fans to stop spinning before opening the server chassis.
Wear full ESD protection: grounded anti-static wrist strap, ESD-safe shoes, and ESD-safe work surface.
Use a thermal camera or infrared thermometer to check for hot spots before touching components.
Document all LED indicator states on the front panel AND rear ports before removing any components.
